note: The adversarial completeness/quality rubric the project must clear before a run is DONE (ESV §B). Each dimension is scored 0-5 by an INDEPENDENT critic subagent; `threshold` is the minimum to pass. `probe` is the generative question the critic asks to find missing work. Pure data — tune the bar here, no code change, no hardcoded methodology vocabulary.

dimensions.probe: Is the problem space genuinely explored across distinct angles AND the premise interrogated (a disconfirmation council)? Which angle is still unexplored?
Which persona SEGMENTS were never sampled in any council? An unsampled segment is missing breadth.
Are there non-obvious concepts (≥1 unexpected mechanism, ≥1 experienceable model, ≥1 dark-horse), or only incremental forms? Which lens is unused?
Are claims grounded in real councils + GROUNDED proband sessions, not asserted? Which claim/prototype is untested?
Did it keep deliberate non-targets + honest open risks, or rationalize the brief? Where is it over-claiming?
Was the winning concept iterated on real feedback across the fidelity ladder, or one-shot? Which fidelity rung / refinement is missing?
Is the project organized (sections), concluded (a substantial solution-presentation), and handed-off (a report)?
